title: Catch Up on Marketing Activities
summary: Joe Furnari and Michael Donovan reviewed positive social media feedback. Paulo Trovao highlighted data injection. Michael Donovan outlined a 'BDC ecosystem' content package. Michael Donovan presented initial Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) work, leading Joe Furnari to approve a $349 monthly subscription for automation. Joe Furnari relayed Vinnie's decision to hire an external ABM firm, expressing concerns about channel conflict and data attribution. Michael Donovan presented a BDC self-assessment app as an MQL engine, with a two-week plan to integrate it and generate leads via email and social campaigns using new ICP data.

decisions.description: Approve $349 monthly subscription for ICP automation.
Integrate BDC self-assessment lead magnet into current site and social rotation.
Focus on email, social media, and landing pages for lead generation for two weeks.

actionItems.task: Push Vinnie for reporting on external ABM firm's targets and details.
Push Jason to provide feedback on social media posts.
Send Michael Donovan an outline of concerns and recommendations regarding the external firm.
Put together a two-week plan for email, social media, landing pages, and driving leads.

expectedOutcome.description: Workflow mapped out, shared, and designed into a calendar.

actionItems.task: Charge the monthly subscription for the ICP platform to his credit card.
Send an email to Michael Donovan regarding the payment for the subscription.

tensions: Lack of direct interaction (comments/likes) on social media posts.
Vinnie's decision to hire an external ABM firm without internal team input.
Concerns about channel conflict, messaging drift, and data attribution with external firm.
Vinnie's refusal to entertain a unified strategy between internal and external teams.
Michael Donovan's frustration over past marketing strategy inconsistencies and lack of resources.
Disagreement on the timeline for BDC app integration (hours vs. days/week).
Debate on whether the BDC self-assessment app is 'step one' or 'step two/three' for lead generation.
referencesToPast: Vinnie's earlier requests for social media posts.
Previous discussions about the 'Never Miss' slogan.
Michael Donovan's prior meeting with Jason.
Vinnie's decision to hire an external ABM firm based on a referral.
Michael Donovan's concerns about marketing strategy since September.
Reference to Q2 last year's record-breaking quarter.

contributions.description: Acknowledged positive impact of recent social media posts, satisfying Vinnie's requests.
Noted injecting posts with thought-provoking data to create a sense of need.
Outlined next steps for developing an integrated 'BDC ecosystem' content package.
Presented initial work on ICP, detailing manual data enrichment due to free plan limitations.
Relayed Vinnie's decision to hire an external ABM firm, expressing concerns.
Recommended a unified strategy between internal team and external ABM firm.
Presented a BDC self-assessment application to serve as an MQL engine.
Proposed a two-week plan focusing on email, social, and landing pages for lead generation.
